exemplar.title: How can Netflix achieve an ideal combination of both its  production of original content and license acquisitions of movies and series to achieve an optimal profit for the next five years?

detailedExemplarData.title: How can Netflix achieve an ideal combination of both its  production of original content and license acquisitions of movies and series to achieve an optimal profit for the next five years?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Explicit selection of the key concept âchangeâ and consistent reference across introduction, each analytical tool, and conclusion.
Use of precise Netflix data (subscriber numbers, market share, content spend) to ground all discussions in the company context.
Decision tree analysis explicitly framed around change, demonstrating clear integration of the key concept into quantitative evaluation.
weaknesses: Lacks exploration of change theories (e.g., Lewinâs or Kotterâs models) to deepen theoretical integration.
SWOT entries are not always linked back to the theme of change, missing opportunities for fuller conceptual integration.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates a strong understanding of the key concept of change, with clear analytical linkage to Netflixâs context and repeated thematic framing. However, it stops short of fully integrating change theory throughout and misses some opportunities to tie qualitative analysis back to the core concept, placing it squarely at band 4.

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents are cited and cross-referenced, meeting the numerical requirement.
Documents are relevant to Netflixâs content strategy and underpin SWOT, decision-tree, and regression analyses.
weaknesses: Sources are predominantly journalistic or blog-style, lacking the academic depth expected for the top band.
Range of ideas is somewhat narrow (mainly quality concerns and cost data), and source-selection criteria are not detailed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the correct number of documents (3â5) is provided and they are generally relevant, the lack of diverse, in-depth academic sources and limited methodological rigour in source selection constrain this criterion to band 3.

strengths: Effective selection of three syllabus-recognised tools (SWOT, decision tree, linear regression) covering strategic, quantitative, and statistical perspectives.
Clear strategic framing in SWOT and differentiation of licensing versus originals.
weaknesses: Applications rely heavily on unsubstantiated assumptions (e.g., success probabilities, revenue per series).
Derivation of key probability estimates in the decision tree is omitted, weakening relevance to the research question.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The tools chosen are well-suited to the research question and demonstrate deliberate breadth. However, inconsistent justification of assumptions and some unclear relevance in application limit the overall performance to band 3.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Data drawn from multiple sources (Statista, Netflix reports, Parrot Analytics) provides a basic evidence base.
The summary interpretation correctly identifies that originals drive subscriber growth.
weaknesses: Regression analysis uses only a few data points and omits reporting of RÂ² and p-values, limiting assessment of reliability.
Analysis lacks depth: confounding factors are not explored, sensitivity analysis is absent, and logical leaps are made without adequate critique.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis shows an adequate selection of data and some linkage to the research question, but overall remains superficial, with limited critique of assumptions and missing key statistical validations, placing it at band 2.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented throughout the IA.
Explicitly answers the research question by recommending a mix of originals and licensing strategies.
weaknesses: Does not address the assumption of constant licensing fees or suggest collecting real-time fee data for future research.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions draw logically from the analyses, maintaining consistency with evidence and explicitly addressing the research question, fulfilling all descriptors for band 3.

strengths: IA follows the prescribed structure (title page, contents, numbered sections, appendices) and uses headings and tables effectively.
Logical progression of sections facilitates readability.
weaknesses: Minor layout issues (some cramped tables) slightly impede visual clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work maintains an appropriate, consistent structure throughout, matching the highest descriptor for this criterion.

strengths: All required presentation elements are present (title information, word count, table of contents, references, appendices).
weaknesses: Minor citation inconsistencies (e.g., âSD 1 SD, 2â caption) reduce overall polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all required elements clearly and comprehensively, satisfying the top band despite a few small consistency issues.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows the prescribed structureâtitle page, contents, numbered sections, appendicesâfacilitating logical flow.  Headings and tables are used effectively.  Minor layout issues (some cramped tables) do not impede readability, so the work matches âappropriate structure throughoutâ (band 2).

The SD articles, annual reports and industry statistics are clearly relevant to Netflixâs content strategy and give enough depth to support SWOT, Decision Tree and Regression.  However, most sources are journalistic or blog-style rather than academic, and the range is confined mainly to quality concerns and cost data, limiting depth.  This aligns with âdocuments are relevant and sufficiently in-depthâ (band 3) rather than the richer range expected for band 4.
All required elements (title information, word count, table of contents, references, appendix, supporting documents) are present.  Hence the strand achieves the top presentation band (2 marks).

Exactly five supporting documents (SD1-SD5) are cited and cross-referenced, meeting the required 3â5 range.  Band 4 is awarded because the maximum in this strand is triggered once the numerical requirement is satisfied and no further qualitative distinctions are provided.

Each tool is applied to Netflix, and the relevance to the research question is generally clear (e.g., regression links content spend to subscribers).  Nonetheless, several applications rely heavily on unsubstantiated assumptions (made-up success probabilities, constant licence fees) which weakens effectiveness.  This places the work in band 3: ârelevance not always clear but adequately applied.â

Data are drawn from Statista, Netflix reports and Parrot Analytics, covering spending, subscriber numbers and demand shareâan **adequate** range.  Yet many numeric inputs (e.g., revenue per series) are inferred rather than sourced, and only five data points are used in each regression.  This reflects âadequate selection and use of data,â matching band 3.

The analysis explains outcomes of each tool and links them to the RQ, showing some integration (e.g., reconciling regression with decision-tree findings).  However, logical leaps (negative correlation of licensing automatically meaning less appeal) and limited critique of assumptions reduce depth.  This meets âsome analysis of the research questionâ but stops short of âmostly effective analysis,â so band 3 is appropriate.

The research question is restated and answered explicitly in the conclusion and throughout the analytical discussion.  Therefore, the strand meets the highest descriptor: âexplicitly answers research question,â band 3.

The IA uses three different toolsâSWOT (qualitative), Decision Tree (quantitative/strategic) and Simple Linear Regression (quantitative/statistical).  Together they cover strategic, financial and statistical perspectives and are widely recognised in the syllabus, showing an effective and deliberate selection suited to the RQ.  This fulfils the âeffective selection of tools and theoriesâ descriptor (band 4).

The conclusion directly reflects the findings of SWOT, decision tree and regression, recommending fewer, higher-quality originals plus more licensing, and notes limitations (monitor external factors).  No contradictions with presented evidence are detected, satisfying âconclusions fully consistent with evidence.â Band 3.

All discussion is rooted in Netflixâs real figures (subscriber numbers, content spend, market share) and issues (quality control, licence fees).  Each tool is applied directly to Netflix and not to a generic firm, showing strong analytical linkage.  Nevertheless, some assumptions are very speculative (e.g., success rates, revenue per episode) and occasionally detach from verifiable company data, so the integration is not seamless across the whole IA.  This fits the descriptor of **strong analysis with partial integration**, i.e., band 4.

The student explicitly selects the key concept of **change** and refers back to it in each analytical tool (e.g., Decision Tree is framed as indicating whether a change in content mix is required).  References to âchange in strategyâ appear in the introduction, the discussion of every tool and in the conclusion.  However, the exploration of how change theory itself (incremental vs transformational, Lewin, Kotter, etc.) would influence Netflix is missing, so the analysis is not fully integrated throughout.  This demonstrates a strong understanding with partial integration, placing it solidly in band 4 rather than the fully-integrated band 5.

annotations.comment: The negative correlation for licensing is stated without exploring potential confounding factors or time trends that could explain the result.
Synthesizing regression findings with SWOT and decision-tree results here would provide a more integrated cross-tool evaluation.
The summary effectively concludes that originals drive subscriber growth while licensing complements library diversity.
The conclusion logically follows from the analyses and explicitly addresses the research question, maintaining consistency with evidence.
Acknowledge the assumption of constant licensing fees in your conclusion and suggest collecting real-time fee data as an area for further research.
The opening paragraph skillfully grounds the discussion with precise market share and subscriber data, effectively establishing Netflixâs organizational context.
The second paragraph introduces âsuccessâ without defining measurable criteria, making subsequent assumptions hard to evaluate consistently.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Consider detailing your source-selection criteria and assessing the reliability of each secondary source to strengthen methodological rigour.
The introductory SWOT overview frames internal and external factors clearly, providing a concise strategic snapshot.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Revise the citation âSD 1 SD, 2â in the SWOT table caption for consistency and clarity in presentation.
The discussion contrasts permanent originals with temporary licensing well, demonstrating clear strategic differentiation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The claim that âmost originals received negative reviewsâ is unsupported by quantitative evidence, weakening the argumentâs credibility.
To deepen the SWOT analysis, consider quantifying viewer fatigue by referencing percentages of negative reviews from your supporting documents.
Linking licensed content to market expansion and content gaps provides valuable directional insight into strategic opportunities.
Expand this insight by specifying target regions or genres for expansion using industry data to support your recommendations.
SWOT entries are not linked back to the key concept of change, missing an opportunity to integrate the core theme throughout the qualitative analysis.
The interpretation of decision tree outcomes clearly connects profit scenarios to strategic adjustments in content investment.
Recommendations refer to âhigh successâ without specifying the underlying probability thresholds, which reduces precision.
Clarify the specific success probabilities from the appendix to strengthen the link between assumptions and decision-tree outcomes.
The regression section acknowledges data aggregation limitations, demonstrating awareness of potential confounding variables.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The decision tree description rightly acknowledges reliance on assumptions but omits how those probability estimates were derived.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Incorporating a sensitivity analysis would illustrate how varying success-probability assumptions alter the decision treeâs strategic recommendation.
Framing the decision tree analysis explicitly around âchangeâ demonstrates effective integration of the key concept into quantitative evaluation.
The regression analysis omits reporting of RÂ² and statistical significance, limiting assessment of model reliability.
Include and interpret RÂ² and p-values to validate the regression results and strengthen your quantitative evaluation.
Reporting the strong positive correlation (r = 0.916) for originals demonstrates effective use of statistical measures.
